pi_items.source: Verified against setup_task.sh and export_result.sh; consistent with task description
Mathematical verification: D = V_supply × T where V_rel = (14-10) = 4 kts, T = 4nm/4kts = 1hr, D = 14 × 1 = 14.0nm
Mathematical verification: D = 10 kts × (30 min / 60 min/hr) = 10 × 0.5 = 5.0nm
Specified in task.json metadata and used consistently in verifier.py for tolerance checks
Task description states 'USS Guide: Heading 000, Speed 10 knots'; verified in verifier.py line 69
Task description states 'Leg 1 (Approach): Speed 14 knots'; verified in verifier.py line 14 (V_sup = 14 kts)
Task description states 'Leg 2 (Station): Speed 10 knots (matching Ownship)'; verified in verifier.py line 19
Task description states 'Start position 4.0nm directly South (astern) of Ownship'; verified in verifier.py line 13 (D_sep = 4 nm)
Task description states 'Duration: 30 minutes'; verified in verifier.py line 20 (Time = 30 mins = 0.5 hours)

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims in this RAS kinematics task are mathematically verified and consistent with the task logic. The task requires calculating geographic distances for a supply ship performing a real-world naval Replenishment at Sea (RAS) maneuver with realistic speeds (10 knots base, 14 knots approach). The approach distance of 14.0 nm is correct: with relative closing speed of 4 knots and 4 nm initial gap, the supply ship travels 14 nm in 1 hour. The station leg distance of 5.0 nm is correct: 10 knots × 0.5 hours = 5.0 nm. The starting position of 49.9333°N (4 nm south of 50.00°N) is correctly specified for latitude calculations.
